description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a solid, metallic head. The handle is made of light-colored wood, adorned with dark printed markings and symbols, and ends in a rounded grip for secure handling. The axe head is forged from steel, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end, suitable for splitting or chopping. The blade is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fitting, visible at the top of the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible grain on the wood and a matte, slightly rough finish on the metal. The markings on the handle suggest branding or safety information, adding to its authenticity as a tool commonly found in urban garages or maintenance sheds.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle featuring dark markings and a broad steel blade, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The handle has a rounded grip and the blade is securely wedged onto the handle.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad steel blade, with visible markings along the handle.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the curvature of the handle and the wedge-shaped blade, with the grip and top of the axe head visible.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the straight alignment of the handle and the rectangular profile of the axe head.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the end of the handle and the underside of the axe head, showing the wedged mounting.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a rounded grip, oval cross-section, visible grain, and printed markings for grip and branding.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll, straight edge, and no beard; matte finish with visible mounting wedge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle and secured by a visible wedge.
